description_long: This house features a long rectangular footprint with a prominent gable roof, half of which is covered in traditional shingles and the other half in a darker, likely metal or tile material. The front facade is defined by a covered porch supported by multiple square columns, providing a shaded entryway and outdoor sitting area. The exterior walls are painted a light pastel green, complemented by white trim and a wooden front door. Windows are arranged asymmetrically, with a large window on one side and smaller windows along the length of the building. The landscaping includes potted plants and a small garden bed near the porch, adding a touch of greenery. The overall design suggests a blend of Colonial and Ranch architectural influences, suitable for suburban or semi-urban neighborhoods.
description: A single-story house with a gable roof, covered porch supported by columns, light green walls, and asymmetrical windows. The roof features both shingle and dark panel sections, with landscaping at the front.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long gable roof, a large window, and the main door with a small human figure for scale.
description_view_1: Rear or top-down view highlighting the full length of the gable roof and the plain back wall with no visible windows or doors.
description_view_2: Angled view of the front corner, showcasing the porch, columns, entryway, and some potted plants.
description_view_3: Full front view displaying the entire porch, multiple columns, main door, windows, and landscaping details.
attribute_entryway_design: Wooden door with a covered porch, multiple square columns, and minimal decorative elements
